picks.narrative: The model sees COL as a 42.0% side; the market is pricing 33.2%, leaving a 9.2pp edge. The model isn't leaning on a marquee matchup here — no single baseball factor rises to a headline; the disagreement is with the posted price itself. The main pushback is home field (~-1.1pp): LAD's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road. With a headwind that size, this is not a clean all-signal read. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +205 still sits inside the playable range (to +163). The read here is the price, not the profile — the model just makes this a better side than the posted number does.

picks.narrative: IBP values SF 6.8pp higher than the market — 56.3% model vs 49.5% implied. Several smaller signals stack the same way — home field (~+0.8pp), pitcher quality (xfip) (~+0.8pp) — with no single one dominating. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +102 still sits inside the playable range (to -110). As a cluster edge rather than one dominant mismatch, it is a thinner, more fragile signal — worth sizing accordingly.

picks.narrative: Model and market disagree on STL by 5.2pp — 52.6% fair against a 47.1% price. The largest lift is defense (oaa) (~+0.6pp): STL defense grades +19 OAA better on the season — a run-prevention edge. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +111 still sits inside the playable range (to +106). This is value in the number more than the matchup — the model just makes STL a better side than the price does.

picks.narrative: The model prices WSH at 56.0% to win where the no-vig market implies 52.2%, a 4.0pp disagreement. No individual baseball factor clears the bar for a headline here — this is not a single visible mismatch, it is the model pricing the side differently than the market. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current -108 still sits inside the playable range (to -108). This isn't a clean all-signal agreement — it's the model calling the posted number too generous for the true win probability.
